connectivity.integrationProtocol: ADPRO SDK (for both Cloud and Local) TCP Receiver (events)
connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Configuration needed on Device. TCP Push to Genesis ADPRO Receiver
connectivity.customerPorts: RTSP Port:554 Control port:2000 Audio Port: 3000
connectivity.genesisPorts: Genesis Adpro Receiver: IP Address: (specific to tenant) Port 10000

comments.currentIssues: * AudioFiles to Play mantained as Custom Settings * No time based Alarm Image pull available * Need TCP Port for SDK communication and RTSP Port for streaming purposes * Server Unit ID is used to identify the device which is an un-reliable integer number which has a high chance of duplication
comments.dependencies: * Video Streaming through Media Stream framework
comments.commonIssues: * Inactive/Disarmed devices alarms will still be forwarded to EventIngest * Event Log pulling is not available in any of the proxies * Bi-directional look-up need to be rock solid (genesis device id to device hub/camera unique id/channel id) * Alarm polling is not available for most of the device types. for existing device types that support polling, its design is not scalable, which causes too many server failures

connectivity.integrationProtocol: Http ISAPI (for Cloud) .NET HcNetSDK (for Local) ISAPI Mutipart callback (events)
connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: No Configuration needed. ISAPI Event API Subscription.
connectivity.loginSecurity: Operator Permissions, ISAPI protocol must be enabled.
connectivity.customerPorts: Control Port: 8000 HTTP Port: 80/HTTPS Port: 443 RTSP Port: 554

comments.currentIssues: * IP Camera timezone conflict in firmware * Unreliable RTSP streaming * Unreliable Heartbeat callback * ISAPI timeout is not handled properly * Require admin-equivalent priviledges to receive alarms * Needs TCP Port for two way audio, Http/s for rest of the functions and RTSP for streaming (No single Port access) * No Camera Unique Identifier avaialble in device level * Device alarm images and device metadata should be used for alarm processing * No device level arm/disarm

connectivity.integrationProtocol: Http HcNetSDK (for Cloud) .NET HcNetSDK (for Local) ISAPI Mutipart callback (events)
connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: No Configuration needed. ISAPI Event API Subscription.
connectivity.loginSecurity: Operator Permissions, ISAPI protocol must be enabled.
connectivity.customerPorts: Control Port: 8000 HTTP Port: 80/HTTPS Port: 443 RTSP Port: Nil

comments.currentIssues: * IP Camera timezone conflict in firmware * Unreliable RTSP streaming * Unreliable Heartbeat callback * ISAPI timeout is not handled properly * Require admin-equivalent priviledges to receive alarms * Needs TCP Port for two way audio, Http/s for rest of the functions and RTSP for streaming (No single Port access) * No Camera Unique Identifier avaialble in device level * Device alarm images and device metadata should be used for alarm processing * No device level arm/disarm

connectivity.integrationProtocol: Dahua .Net NetSDK (for both Cloud and Local) Dahua .Net Subscription (events)
connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: No Configuration needed on device. Automatically subscribed
connectivity.customerPorts: Port 37777 / need http or https to access web interface

comments.currentIssues: * No Timesync option * No Heartbeat support * Needs TCP Port for all the key functions and RTSP for Streaming purposes (No single Port access) * Require admin-equivalent priviledges for most of the functions * No Camera Unique Identifier avaialble in device level * Device alarm images and device metadata should be used for alarm processing * No device level arm/disarm

connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Manual Configuration of Rules needed on device for every event code using the Immix protocol

comments.currentIssues: * Device alarm images and device metadata should be used for alarm processing * Alarm rule configuration is complex and human error prone. * No device level arm/disarm
comments.dependencies: * RTSP and HTTP Ports are same. So technically, its a single port device

connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Events not supported with current version

comments.notes: This integration was specifically done for Hesta. Features are limited to discovery, live, plaback and timeline to use the video device to look up during reception of intrusion events

connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Manual setup of NXGEN/IMMIX Genesis TCP IP and port needed

comments.currentIssues: * Device metata should be used instead of metadata burnt images from Device Camect needs a dev environment

connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Manual configuration of webhook needed to push to NXGen proxy API
connectivity.customerPorts: Genesis Ganz receiver DNS/Port HTTP(S) Port: 80/443

comments.currentIssues: * Device metata should be used instead of metadata burnt images from Device * Alarm rule configuration in device level is required which is human error prone. * Trigger health is not available

connectivity.integrationProtocol: Avigilon HTTP API (for Cloud), C# SDK (for Local mode) WebHook Callback (event)
connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: No manual configuration needed. Two Options 1. Automatic Webhook registration, 2. Event polling from UI
connectivity.loginSecurity: Standard User Profile - View permissions to Camera config, Control commands,
connectivity.customerPorts: Avigilon: TCP:38880 Avigilon VMS: MT playground must be enabled. Port 8443 Avigilon Appliance: 443

comments.notes: Avigilon does not support an RTSP source

connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: No Manual configuration needed. Automatic websocket subscription to pull events.

comments.currentIssues: * Passwords might not support special characters @, # * Alarm polling is not reliable

connectivity.loginSecurity: Operaotr permissions. Add host entry if necessary

comments.currentIssues: * SDK based non-scalable alarm subscription * SDK tied to Smart Client * SDK has multi feature access issues for a device. Every action needs to be sequenced

comments.notes: Axis Camera station does not support RTSP
comments.currentIssues: * So far, no test device to test
* Legacy. Need to suggest customers to replace Heitel with some other device types

comments.notes: Thermal camera is not supported in Healthcheck Premium

connectivity.integrationProtocol: Viasys HTTP API WebHook Callback (API Cusrtomer)
connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Webhook call back from Viasys for event push. Viasys and MrHill API to integrate from Genesis
connectivity.loginSecurity: None. Genesis Device GUID is the authkey

connectivity.eventIntegrationMethod: Webhook configuration from IPCamera rules to push video clip to genesis

connectivity.customerPorts: Mobile Bridge Port: 8433, RTSP Port:8488 Password encrypt using Senstar Password exe
Mobile Bridge Port: 8433, RTSP Port:8488 Password encrypt using Senstar Password exe

comments.notes: Cloud Signalling - AJAX Cloud setup to send SIA DC09 alarms AJAX PIRCAM: Setup AJAX cloud signalling for sending AJAX PIR Cam images AJAX IP CAM: (photo mode) AJAX Beta for AJAX Cam to send 3 images. AJAX NVR: uses 3rd party CCTV cameras. photo mode of 3 pictures is NOT supported. this mode sends AJAX cloud URL only.
